reasoning: Initial observation suggests a linear relationship between position, velocity, and time. To confirm this, I will increase the time and observe the change in position.

Increasing time from 2.0 to 3.0 seconds resulted in an increase in position from 10.0 to 15.0 meters, which supports the hypothesis of a linear relationship. To further confirm the relationship, I will now increase the velocity and observe the change in position.

The position has doubled when the velocity was doubled while keeping time constant. This supports the hypothesis of a linear relationship between position, velocity, and time. Given the initial conditions and the observations, the equation for position can be formulated as position = velocity * time.
